enterpriseDataPlatform.title: Enterprise Industrial Data Platform
enterpriseDataPlatform.summary: Flagship architecture narrative demonstrating how one codebase supports two operating modes: a local reproducible lakehouse for deterministic validation and an enterprise cloud stack for governed production analytics.

executiveSummary.title: Executive Summary
executiveSummary.body: This project presents a production-oriented data platform that keeps engineering rigor visible in a public repository. The architecture separates experimentation from enterprise operation through a two-mode model, while preserving shared contracts, quality assertions, and operational metadata across both modes.

architectureOverview.title: Architecture Overview (Mode 1 / Mode 2)
architectureOverview.intro: The platform intentionally exposes two execution paths so delivery teams can validate logic locally and then promote the same semantics to an enterprise runtime.
architectureOverview.mode1Body: Runs with local orchestration, Delta-based medallion layers, and fixed sample windows. It is optimized for reproducibility, root-cause analysis, and interview-safe demonstration.
architectureOverview.mode2Body: Runs on managed enterprise services with IAM boundaries, centralized catalog/governance, and team-level observability. It is optimized for compliance, scale, and controlled operations.
architectureOverview.tradeoff: Trade-off: Mode 1 prioritizes deterministic debugging and onboarding speed; Mode 2 prioritizes governance controls, security segregation, and long-run reliability. Shared contracts avoid behavior drift between modes.

governance.title: Governance and Data Contracts
governance.item1: Schema contracts are versioned and validated at ingestion boundaries, preventing silent type drift from propagating downstream.
governance.item2: Data quality rules are treated as release gates: failed expectations route records to quarantine and block promotion to trusted layers.
governance.item3: Contract ownership is explicit by layer (Bronze, Silver, Gold), with accountable producers and consumers documented in runbooks/ADRs.
governance.item4: Lineage and run metadata provide auditability for each published dataset, including window, code revision, and validation outcome.

twoModeArchitecture.title: Two-Mode Architecture (Local Lakehouse + Enterprise Stack)
twoModeArchitecture.item1: Mode 1 mirrors medallion semantics locally: Bronze for raw ingestion, Silver for validated conformance, and Gold for analytical consumption.
twoModeArchitecture.item2: Promotion to Mode 2 preserves model contracts and test logic instead of rewriting business rules per environment.
twoModeArchitecture.item3: Enterprise mode introduces managed orchestration, secrets/IAM policies, governed catalogs, and workload isolation by domain/team.
twoModeArchitecture.item4: A consistent run context (run_id, window, validation status) allows deterministic troubleshooting across local and enterprise executions.

observability.title: Observability and Reliability
observability.item1: Pipeline health is tracked through freshness, volume, and quality SLIs with threshold-based alerting tied to operational ownership.
observability.item2: Failure handling is explicit: retries for transient faults, quarantine for invalid data, and replay flow for deterministic recovery.
observability.item3: Dashboards combine platform metrics (latency, throughput) with data-product metrics (contract pass rate, null ratio, drift deltas).
observability.item4: Runbook procedures standardize incident response, reducing MTTR and avoiding ad-hoc recovery steps under pressure.

cicd.title: CI/CD and Quality Gates
cicd.item1: CI validates formatting, static checks, unit tests, and contract-focused transformations before any deployment artifact is promoted.
cicd.item2: Data tests (dbt + expectation suites) run as mandatory gates and fail the pipeline when contract criteria are violated.
cicd.item3: Artifact promotion is stage-based with explicit approvals, preserving traceability from commit to execution window.
cicd.item4: Release discipline prioritizes deterministic outcomes over speed, reducing rollback cost in regulated or high-impact contexts.

adrs.title: Architectural Decisions (ADRs)
adrs.item1: ADR-001: Adopt medallion layering to enforce progressive trust boundaries and simplify downstream consumption contracts.
adrs.item2: ADR-002: Keep a deterministic local mode to shorten feedback loops and guarantee reproducible engineering evidence.
adrs.item3: ADR-003: Encode data quality as executable contracts rather than optional documentation.
adrs.item4: ADR-004: Standardize run metadata and lineage capture to improve auditability and cross-team incident analysis.

reproducibility.title: Reproducibility Commands
reproducibility.intro: The command sequence below is used to bootstrap dependencies, execute deterministic windows, and validate contract gates end-to-end.
reproducibility.note: In enterprise mode, the same logical steps are executed via managed orchestration, preserving contract parity with local runs.

recruiterProof.title: Recruiter Proof Section
recruiterProof.item1: Public repository with executable pipeline paths and operational documentation for independent verification.
recruiterProof.item2: Evidence of architecture reasoning through ADRs, governance boundaries, and quality gate definitions.
recruiterProof.item3: Clear trade-off articulation between deterministic local delivery and enterprise production constraints.
recruiterProof.item4: Measurable artifacts (lineage, quality checks, run metadata) ready for technical interview deep dives.

whatItShows.title: What it shows

whatItShows.item1: Operational Command View for fleet health, alarms, tenant context, and device command workflows.
whatItShows.item2: Operational Observability Dashboard for ingestion latency, throughput, and processing-state visibility.
whatItShows.item3: Unified operator context for command execution and real-time diagnostics during incidents.
whatItShows.item4: Practical enterprise UX focused on critical decisions instead of vanity metrics.

whatItSolves.title: What it solves
whatItSolves.item1: Reduces mean-time-to-detect and mean-time-to-recover for telemetry and command-path incidents.
whatItSolves.item2: Improves reliability for multi-tenant industrial operations with strict isolation boundaries.
whatItSolves.item3: Creates a single operational truth across telemetry ingestion, command dispatch, and alerting.
whatItSolves.item4: Supports predictable scale-out from pilot facilities to enterprise multi-site deployments.

executiveSummary.title: Executive Summary
executiveSummary.body: Flagship enterprise architecture for telemetry-heavy operations. The platform is event-driven, multi-tenant, and designed for high-throughput ingestion with resilient downstream analytics.

architecture.title: Architecture
architecture.item1: Modbus polling and protocol adapters ingest field telemetry into a Kafka-centered event backbone.
architecture.item2: Processing services enrich and route telemetry to OLTP workflows and Timescale time-series analytics.
architecture.item3: Command APIs are decoupled from ingestion paths, preserving resilience under burst traffic.
architecture.item4: Replay and backfill flows support deterministic recovery for missed windows and downstream outages.

securityGovernance.title: Security & Governance
securityGovernance.item1: Role-based access controls enforce tenant boundaries for operators, administrators, and integrators.
securityGovernance.item2: Command and data-access actions are audit-traced with actor, timestamp, and scope metadata.
securityGovernance.item3: Secrets and connector credentials are isolated per environment and integration boundary.
securityGovernance.item4: Operational governance uses runbooks, ownership mapping, and incident/postmortem protocols.

evidence.title: Evidence
evidence.item1: Operational runbooks for restart, replay, and command-path incident containment.
evidence.item2: Alert packs tied to ingestion lag, processing backlogs, and tenant-level error budgets.
evidence.item3: Production dashboards proving throughput, latency, and service-level behavior over time.

enterpriseDataPlatform.title: Enterprise Industrial Data Platform
enterpriseDataPlatform.summary: Backend-focused enterprise case showing how data flow, SLAs, contracts, quality gates, and observability become public delivery evidence.

seo.title: Enterprise Industrial Data Platform Case Study | Phaiffer Tech
seo.description: Backend case study with architecture diagram, data flow, SLAs, contracts, quality gates, observability signals, and public evidence artifacts.

whatItShows.title: What it shows
whatItShows.item1: A production-minded industrial data platform designed with explicit trust boundaries from ingestion to analytics.
whatItShows.item2: Deterministic portfolio exports and machine-readable manifests suitable for customer-facing sales enablement.
whatItShows.item3: Contract-first data delivery discipline, with quality controls as mandatory release gates.
whatItShows.item4: Operational evidence artifacts that hiring teams and customers can verify directly in the public repository.

whatItSolves.title: What it solves
whatItSolves.item1: Inconsistent data handoffs between ingestion and analytics teams.
whatItSolves.item2: Low trust in KPI outputs caused by weak quality enforcement and undocumented assumptions.
whatItSolves.item3: Difficult incident triage due to missing run context and fragmented observability signals.
whatItSolves.item4: Poor customer communication when backend projects lack clear demonstration artifacts.

architecture.title: Architecture
architecture.subtitle: Mermaid snapshot of the platform operating model used for portfolio demonstrations.
architecture.item1: Layered flow from Bronze ingestion to Silver standardization and Gold data products.
architecture.item2: Quality gates and observability wired into promotion paths, not treated as afterthoughts.
architecture.item3: Reproducible run context with run_id, timestamps, row counts, and DQ outcomes.
architecture.item4: Portfolio report generation isolated from raw data to keep public artifacts lightweight.

dataFlow.title: Data flow
dataFlow.item1: Source records are ingested into Bronze with ingestion metadata and immutable traceability fields.
dataFlow.item2: Silver applies type normalization, deduplication, and schema conformance checks.
dataFlow.item3: Gold publishes business-facing data products and KPI-ready tables.
dataFlow.item4: Portfolio export job reads curated outputs and emits website-ready evidence packs.

slas.title: SLAs
slas.item1: Stage runtime SLAs are monitored and reported in each portfolio run manifest.
slas.item2: Freshness expectations are defined for promoted datasets before stakeholder consumption.
slas.item3: Alert thresholds target latency regressions and stalled processing windows.
slas.item4: SLA compliance signals are captured as part of delivery readiness evidence.

contracts.title: Data contracts
contracts.item1: Schema contracts are enforced at ingestion and transformation boundaries.
contracts.item2: Layer ownership is explicit across Bronze, Silver, and Gold responsibilities.
contracts.item3: Contract changes are reviewable and traceable through versioned project history.
contracts.item4: Contract outcomes are visible in CI and run artifacts for auditability.

qualityGates.title: Quality gates
qualityGates.item1: dbt tests run as release gates for keys, relationships, and semantic assumptions.
qualityGates.item2: Great Expectations checkpoints enforce completeness, validity, and allowed-value rules.
qualityGates.item3: Violation counts are summarized in run manifests and exported quality charts.
qualityGates.item4: Promotion paths require passing quality checks before downstream publication.

observabilitySignals.title: Observability signals
observabilitySignals.item1: Pipeline runtime, throughput, and freshness metrics are captured per execution stage.
observabilitySignals.item2: Data quality outcomes are published as explicit validation statistics.
observabilitySignals.item3: Export jobs track artifact sizes and generation metadata for reproducibility.
observabilitySignals.item4: Operational status is packaged as evidence for technical due diligence.

securityGovernance.title: Security & Governance
securityGovernance.item1: No secrets or private datasets are committed in public evidence outputs.
securityGovernance.item2: Repository guidance defines boundaries for public artifacts versus generated runtime data.
securityGovernance.item3: Governance narratives explain ownership, controls, and release criteria.
securityGovernance.item4: PII handling guidance is documented for enterprise adaptation.

evidenceArtifacts.title: Evidence artifacts
evidenceArtifacts.repoDescription: Complete source tree with reproducible commands and architecture context.
evidenceArtifacts.runbookDescription: Execution and operating procedures for the reproducible pipeline flow.
evidenceArtifacts.contractsDescription: Schema and validation contract documentation used by the platform.
evidenceArtifacts.productionPathDescription: Deployment and operating-mode guidance from local validation to enterprise runtime.
evidenceArtifacts.portfolioScriptDescription: Deterministic chart and manifest generator used for website-ready evidence packs.
evidenceArtifacts.ciDescription: Automated validation path including linting and portfolio generation checks.

demoAssetsStrategy.title: Demo assets strategy
demoAssetsStrategy.subtitle: Why backend projects are presented through verifiable engineering evidence instead of fake front-end visuals.
demoAssetsStrategy.item1: Architecture diagrams communicate design decisions and system boundaries clearly.
demoAssetsStrategy.item2: Runbooks capture operational procedures and incident-response discipline.
demoAssetsStrategy.item3: Tests and quality gates prove reliability claims through executable checks.
demoAssetsStrategy.item4: Sample queries and dataset lineage show practical analytics usability.
demoAssetsStrategy.item5: CI checks provide repeatable proof that the project remains healthy over time.
demoAssetsStrategy.item6: Portfolio manifests make each demo run auditable and reproducible for stakeholders.

deterministicEvidence.title: Evidence / Artifacts
deterministicEvidence.summary: All visuals are generated from deterministic artifacts instead of manually edited slides, so clients and recruiters can verify claims independently.
deterministicEvidence.artifacts: Primary sources include dbt manifest and run_results files, Great Expectations data docs, and exported lineage/report bundles.
deterministicEvidence.item1: Medallion transitions are traceable by model ownership and promotion status across Raw, Bronze, Silver, and Gold.
deterministicEvidence.item2: Orchestration metadata links each run to code revision, execution window, and validation outcomes.
deterministicEvidence.item3: Lineage captures upstream and downstream dependencies for auditable incident triage and impact analysis.
deterministicEvidence.item4: Quality checkpoints expose pass/fail counts and quarantine behavior before business-facing publication.
